title: Small talk in the lift
description: Chat briefly with your neighbour on the way up in the building lift
context: Most Hong Kongers live in high-rise apartments and share a lift with dozens of neighbours. Lift encounters are short and friendly — just enough to be neighbourly without overstaying your welcome. A smile and basic pleasantries go a long way.
culturalFact: In Cantonese culture, 'Have you eaten yet?' (食咗飯未？) is a common greeting that doubles as genuine concern for your wellbeing. Answering truthfully is fine — it's both small talk and a check-in.

lines.speaker: you
lines.jyutping: zou2 san4! sik6 zo2 faan6 mei6?
lines.english: Good morning! Have you eaten yet?

lines.speaker: you
lines.cjk: 仲未，等陣去飲茶。天氣好好喎，今日。
lines.jyutping: zung6 mei6, dang2 zan6 heoi3 jam2 caa4. tin1 hei3 hou2 hou2 wo3, gam1 jat6.
lines.english: Not yet, I'm going for yum cha shortly. Nice weather today.

lines.speaker: neighbour
lines.jyutping: hai6 aa3, ceot1 heoi3 haang4 haa5 lo3. ni1 cang4?
lines.english: Yes, good day for a walk. This floor?
